Dockerfile.general_idea: Update the base image from a Java 11 distribution to a Java 21 distribution to align with the project's JDK upgrade.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The application code has been migrated to Java 21 features and APIs; running it on a Java 11 runtime would result in runtime errors (e.g., NoSuchMethodError, UnsupportedClassVersionError) or failure to start.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java source and target version property from 11 to 21 to align with the JDK 21 migration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 11, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility checks.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade JaCoCo version to 0.8.14 to ensure compatibility with Java 21 class files.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older JaCoCo versions (like 0.8.5) do not support the class file version generated by Java 21, causing runtime errors during code coverage execution.
